Socialtrait Meeting minutes 2026-07-08 Confidential · internal

Product update — monthly sync, July

Monthly product review: June releases, active development, and go-to-market readiness for the CX simulator.

TL;DR Five features shipped in June; the CX simulator needs a different buyer profile and revenue-first messaging before wider client outreach.
When
2026-07-08 · 14:00–15:00 AEST
Where
Google Meet (recorded)
Organizer
Devika R
Minutes
Ori agent · reviewed by Devika R
Present
Devika R, Vikram S, Gemma L, Saanvi T
Apologies
Tomas B (recording shared)
Doc ID
ST-MIN-20260708
Recording
drive.google.com/…/rec-0708

Agenda

  1. 01June releases and monthly update process
  2. 02Features in active development
  3. 03Product catalog and roadmap snapshot
  4. 04CX simulator: buyers, collateral, packaging

01Discussion

June shipped five features; four more are in flight

Devika walked through the June releases. The simulator's ignore list lets client engineering exclude known-unsupported failure modes from the health score, so launch-readiness reflects actual product scope. The context-brief upload now auto-generates research objectives and ranking parameters (previously a support-team task), and objective generation was optimized from ~2 minutes to 30–40 seconds after client feedback.

Private workspaces

Organization switching shipped in response to an enterprise client's insights team needing a workspace invisible to sibling teams. Users can now belong to multiple organizations and switch between them.

In development

Workflow-driven user journeys, raised asset-upload limits (10 assets, 5 videos, 7 MB images), and the paused polls migration — targeted around end of July.

Socialtrait / Ori · ST-MIN-20260708 01 / 02
Socialtrait Meeting minutes ST-MIN-20260708

02Discussion

The CX simulator sells to a different buyer than our core product

Gemma asked how the simulator is reaching clients and noted its buyers differ from our usual profile. Vikram agreed: primary users are the engineering, product, and QA teams building the assistant; economic buyers sit with SVP Engineering, CIO, or CPO. Gemma will not push client outreach until there is a clear buyer-identification plan.

Vikram pressed for revenue-first messaging: "improves customer experience" is weaker than conversion lift, guided selling, and ROI — our USP is testing against the client's actual audience at scale. Trial packaging (discounted, monthly, capped-runs, run-based) was discussed; a classic free trial is unlikely given run costs. No pricing decision was made (see A5).

03Decisions

What we agreed

  1. D1Saanvi joins the monthly product-update thread; prior updates shared for context.
  2. D2The product catalog is the first reference for product positioning, use cases, and ICPs.
  3. D3The CX simulator is positioned on revenue impact — conversion, guided selling, ROI — not only assistant quality.
  4. D4Trial and pricing structure stay open; packaging options to be worked before client outreach.

04Actions

Who does what, by when

#ActionOwnerDueStatus
A1Forward current product update to Saanvi; add her to the threadDevika07-10Open
A2Fix catalog access permissions (Gemma blocked)Devika07-10Blocked
A3Share simulator blurb, deck, and consolidated docs with GemmaDevika / Sana07-11Open
A4Review simulator materials through a revenue-impact lens; sync with sales on traction so farGemma07-18Open
A5Draft packaging options: discounted, monthly, capped-runs, run-basedDevika / VikramTBDOpen
A6Check GTM Slack channel access with SanaSaanvi07-09Done

Next meeting: 2026-08-05, monthly product sync — carry-over review of A5 packaging options.
